title: Cymbalaria elatine
intro: Cymbalaria elatine, also known as Kenilworth ivy or ivy-leaved toadflax, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the family Plantaginaceae. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, due to its calming and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Cymbalaria elatine include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Cymbalaria elatine is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and is sometimes used to make teas, syrups, or topical preparations for the treatment of skin conditions.
taxonomy: Cymbalaria elatine is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is a member of the genus Cymbalaria and the order Lamiales. The species is native to Europe and Western Asia. Cymbalaria elatine is commonly known as keeled toadflax or ivy toadflax. It is an annual or biennial herb that grows as a climber or scrambler.
uses: Cymbalaria elatine, also known as keeled alpine forked flower or ivy-leaved toadflax, has been used in traditional medicine due to its potential therapeutic properties. Its leaves and stems contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. In traditional European folk medicine, Cymbalaria elatine has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. However, its medicinal uses are not well-studied, and more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety. Further studies are required to fully understand the potential health benefits of Cymbalaria elatine.
benefits: Cymbalaria elatine, also known as keel-flowered alpine or ivy-leaved toadflax, is a plant that has been studied for its potential health benefits. Its leaves and flowers have been used in herbal medicine for centuries to treat various ailments. properties: Cymbalaria elatine, also known as Alpine ivy or keel over, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties.
